description: Strengthen Character Consistency System: Upgrade PersistentLatentSeed with multi-frame temporal anchoring, add OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER integration into generate_pro() and generate_extended_video(), implement CharacterFeatureExtractor for detailed profile extraction (facial features, body type, hair, age, skin tone, clothing), and add dual-anchor identity persistence across story scenes.
steps: In /projects/sandbox/ltx/LTX_PRO.py, replace the existing PersistentLatentSeed class (around line 1030-1120) with an enhanced version that: (a) supports multi-frame reference (accepts multiple reference images), (b) adds temporal decay with configurable OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER window, (c) computes frequency-domain features from reference for more stable identity preservation, (d) adds a get_identity_noise() method that returns noise shaped for video latents with per-frame decay weighting.

Add a new CharacterFeatureExtractor class after CharacterPromptAnchor (around line 1180). This class takes a character image and extracts a structured profile dict with keys: ethnicity_skin_tone, age_estimate, hair_color_style, body_type, clothing_description, distinguishing_features, facial_features. Use the InlineVisionDescribe model to generate this, with a specialized CHARACTER_EXTRACT_PROMPT that outputs structured key:value pairs.

Enhance the CharacterPromptAnchor class to accept the extracted profile dict and auto-generate a formatted anchor prefix that includes all profile fields. Add a method build_consistency_prefix(profile_dict) that produces: '[Character: {name}. {age}-year-old {ethnicity} {gender}, {hair}, {body_type}, {clothing}, {distinguishing}. Maintain exact appearance throughout.]'

In generate_pro() (around line 4376), in PHASE 3 (CHARACTER ANCHOR), integrate the enhanced PersistentLatentSeed.blend_into_latent() call with OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER as the num_frames parameter. Currently blend_into_latent is defined but never called in the pipeline. Wire it into the latent preparation phase after LTXVImgToVideoInplace so the character noise is blended into the first OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER frames.

In generate_extended_video() (line 5511), add character identity reinforcement between segments: after extracting the anchor frame for the next segment, also blend PersistentLatentSeed noise into the anchor. Add a config option USE_IDENTITY_REINFORCEMENT (default True) that controls whether the persistent seed noise is re-applied at each segment boundary.

Add a new global config variable CHARACTER_PROFILE_EXTRACTION = False with @param annotation in Cell 5 (around line 3980). When True and a CHARACTER_IMAGE_PATH is set, auto-extract the character profile at startup and store it in CHARACTER_EXTRACTED_PROFILE. Wire this into the CharacterPromptAnchor so it uses the full profile instead of just CHARACTER_DESCRIPTION.
acceptance_criteria: python -m py_compile LTX_PRO.py passes without error
The PersistentLatentSeed class has a blend_into_latent method that accepts num_frames parameter and applies temporal decay
A CharacterFeatureExtractor class exists with an extract_profile() method
CharacterPromptAnchor has a build_consistency_prefix() method that uses structured profile data
generate_pro() calls blend_into_latent with OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER when character_mode is 'anchor' or 'both'
generate_extended_video() applies identity reinforcement at segment boundaries
CHARACTER_PROFILE_EXTRACTION config variable exists in Cell 5 section
findings: InlineVisionDescribe has no model_name param in __init__; it takes model_key in describe(). CharacterFeatureExtractor updated to match actual API. load_image_tensor() exists at line ~1898 and works for 4D tensors. The _char_mode variable in generate_pro() is computed from character_mode.lower().strip().
